description:
Container resource for running long-running container workloads.

A Container defines a deployable unit that runs on a ContainerCluster.
Horizon handles scheduling replicas across machines, autoscaling based on
various metrics, and service discovery.

## Example

```rust
use alien_core::{Container, ContainerCode, ResourceSpec, ContainerAutoscaling, ContainerPort, ExposeProtocol};

let container = Container::new("api".to_string())
    .cluster("compute".to_string())
    .code(ContainerCode::Image {
        image: "myapp:latest".to_string(),
    })
    .cpu(ResourceSpec { min: "0.5".to_string(), desired: "1".to_string() })
    .memory(ResourceSpec { min: "512Mi".to_string(), desired: "1Gi".to_string() })
    .port(8080)
    .expose_port(8080, ExposeProtocol::Http)
    .autoscaling(ContainerAutoscaling {
        min: 2,
        desired: 3,
        max: 10,
        target_cpu_percent: Some(70.0),
        target_memory_percent: None,
        target_http_in_flight_per_replica: Some(100),
        max_http_p95_latency_ms: None,
    })
    .permissions("container-execution".to_string())
    .build();
```

desired.description: Initial desired replicas at container creation

maxHttpP95LatencyMs.description: Maximum acceptable p95 HTTP latency in milliseconds

targetCpuPercent.description: Target CPU utilization percentage for scaling (default: 70%)

targetHttpInFlightPerReplica.description: Target in-flight HTTP requests per replica

targetMemoryPercent.description: Target memory utilization percentage for scaling (default: 80%)

cluster.description:
ContainerCluster resource ID that this container runs on.
If None, will be auto-assigned by ContainerClusterMutation at deployment time.

src.description: The source directory to build from

binaryName.description: Name of the binary to build and run

oneOf.description: TypeScript/JavaScript compiled to single executable with Bun

binaryName.description: Name of the compiled binary (defaults to package.json name if not specified)

failureThreshold.description: Number of consecutive failures before marking replica unhealthy

method.description: HTTP method to use for health check

port.description: Port to check (defaults to container port if not specified)

id.description:
Unique identifier for the container.
Must be DNS-compatible: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens.

items.description:
New ResourceRef that works with any resource type.
This can eventually replace the enum-based ResourceRef for full extensibility.

type.description: Resource type identifier that determines the specific kind of resource. This field is used for polymorphic deserialization and resource-specific behavior.

memory.description: Memory resource requirements (must use Ki/Mi/Gi/Ti suffix)

oneOf.description: Persistent storage configuration (only for stateful containers)

iops.description: IOPS (for storage types that support it)

storageType.description: Storage type (e.g., "gp3", "io2" for AWS, "pd-ssd" for GCP)

throughput.description: Throughput in MiB/s (for storage types that support it)

pool.description:
Capacity group to run on (must exist in the cluster)
If not specified, containers are scheduled to any available group.

oneOf.description: Optional exposure protocol (if None, port is internal-only)

ports.description: Container ports to expose (at least one required)

replicas.description: Fixed replica count (for stateful containers or stateless without autoscaling)

stateful.description: Whether container is stateful (gets stable ordinals, optional persistent volumes)
